channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What to know about the debt ceiling debate as a government shutdown looms in Washington
item.description: A debate over the debt ceiling is at the center of a dispute over funding that is pushing Washington to the brink of a federal government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 57 of 108 invalid voters cast ballots in Greenfield Union School District election
item.description: The Greenfield Union School District superintendent confirmed that 57 votes were cast by 108 ineligible voters for the Area C trustee seat.
item.dc:creator: Connor Dore, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden expected to visit Pope Francis in final international trip of his presidency, AP sources say
item.description: President Joe Biden is expected to announce soon that he will visit the Vatican to meet with Pope Francis next month for what would likely be the final international trip of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prosecutor Fani Willis is removed from the Georgia election case against Trump and others
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A state appeals court on Thursday removed Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others, the latest legal victory for the president-elect in criminal cases that once threatened his career and freedom.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what happens during a partial government shutdown
item.description: During a partial government shutdown, nonessential services pause and federal workers face furloughs or delayed pay, but essential operations will continue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. House members want answers on Texas’ decision to not review maternal deaths after near-total abortion ban
item.description: U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett of Dallas led a letter calling for a briefing on why the state won’t review 2022 and 2023 deaths.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: How much power would RFK Jr. have at HHS? A former health secretary weighs in
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is making the case with senators that he should lead Health and Human Services. Kathleen Sebelius, who had the job under Obama, explains the power and limits of the role.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gorenstein, MPR News

item.title: Tim Scott's Senate campaign arm staff revealed ahead of crucial 2026 swing state races
item.description: Sen. Tim Scott is bringing on a slate of GOP veterans with a variety of political experiences as he looks to expand the Republican Senate majority in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State revamps curriculum, bans ‘woke garbage’ to teach all aspects of Obama-Biden-Trump era
item.description: Oklahoma Superintendent of Public Instruction Ryan Walters will ensure wokeness is removed from curriculum about Obama, Trump and Biden particularly, he told Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate passes defense bill that will raise troop pay and aims to counter China's power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a defense bill Wednesday that authorizes significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members, aims to counter China's growing power and boosts overall military spending to $895 billion while also stripping coverage of transgender medical treatments for chil
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Party switch paying off? Valdes secures committee leadership after joining GOP
item.description: One state Representative in the Tampa Bay area made the controversial move to switch political parties, just last week. The news came was greeted with both cheers and criticisms.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams expected in court on criminal charges: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Senate committee to hold confirmation hearing for defense secretary nominee Hegseth on Jan 14
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for Secretary of Defense is expected to face his first public questioning from U.S. senators on Jan. 14. Senate Armed Services Committee Chairman Roger Wicker, R-Miss.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security bill clears first hurdle in Senate
item.description: The Senate voted 73-27 to advance the measure, which could mean higher Social Security benefits for some Americans if passed before Congress leaves this week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Paris Hilton-backed youth welfare bill passes House, heads to Biden's desk
item.description: Today is a day I will never forget. After years of sharing my story and advocating on Capitol Hill, the Stop Institutional Child Abuse Act has officially passed the U.S. Congress,” Hilton said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Senate passes defense bill that will raise troop pay and aims to counter China's power
item.description: The Senate has passed a defense bill that authorize significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members and boost overall military spending to $895 billion. It also strips coverage of transgender medical treatments for children of military members.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Why shooting down mystery drones carries big risks
item.description: The buzz around the mysterious flurry of drones above New Jersey and other nearby states has prompted some officials, including President-elect Trump, to call for the U.S. military to shoot down the mystery objects. But such measures come with major risks, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans butt heads on daylight saving time after Trump directive
item.description: Trump pledged that “The Republican Party will use its best efforts to eliminate Daylight Saving Time"
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says there’s ‘no sense of danger’ from drones
item.description: “Nothing nefarious apparently, but they’re checking it all out,” Biden told reporters about the drones.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Horsford vows to defend Nevada families as priority for government cuts grows
item.description: Nevada Democratic Rep. Steven Horsford is doing more than just serving as a watchdog on Republican initiatives to cut government bureaucracy -- he's participating in the process.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's tariffs threat is dividing business owners across the country
item.description: Businesses are divided over Trump's plan to impose sweeping tariffs. Some companies welcome the protection from foreign competition, while others worry about rising costs and retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News
